idea.hook: Imagine a world where it rains... but not water!
facts: Saturn's moon Titan is the second-largest moon in our solar system, bigger than the planet Mercury.
It's the only moon with a dense atmosphere, thicker than Earth's, composed mostly of nitrogen. Surface temperature is a frigid -290°F (-179°C).
On Titan, rivers, lakes, and seas are filled not with water, but with liquid methane and ethane.
idea.payoff: Titan is the ONLY other body in our solar system with stable surface liquid and an active hydrological cycle!
idea.title: Rain... of Methane?! 🤯🪐

scenes.visual: Starts with an animated Earth with rain falling, then quickly transitions to a distant, hazy Saturn with Titan orbiting.

scenes.text: Titan: Saturn's moon, BIGGER than Mercury!
scenes.visual: A visual comparison showing Titan next to a smaller Mercury, both orbiting a larger Saturn in the background.
A close-up of Titan's orange-brown, hazy surface with visible atmospheric layers. A temperature gauge graphic fades in.

scenes.text: Rivers, Lakes, Seas... of LIQUID METHANE!
scenes.visual: Animated depiction of rivers and lakes on Titan's surface, showing a dark, reflective liquid. Rain particles (methane) are subtly falling.

scenes.text: Titan: ONLY other world with active liquid cycle!
scenes.visual: An awe-inspiring artistic rendering of Titan's landscape at sunset, with methane clouds, rain, and vast liquid hydrocarbon lakes. The overall scene feels vibrant and active.
